countries: Australia (Cocos-Keeling and Christmas Islands, Great Barrier Reef)
nativeRange.habitat: Hard substrate of clear lagoon and seaward reefs; typically found from the surface to about 67 m, with adults commonly observed on shallow reef flats and slopes.

decorPreferences: highly oxygenated water with strong flow

diet.notes: Has been documented grazing primarily on filamentous and turf algae in the wild. In aquaria, generally advised to offer dried marine seaweed (nori) on a veggie clip at least three times per week, supplemented with spirulina-based prepared foods. Has been known to accept meaty foods such as mysis and brine alongside community feedings, but a vegetable-leaning diet tends to support immune health and reduce HLLE risk.

summary: An Indo-Pacific surgeonfish with a velvety dark body, distinctive white cheek patch, and yellow caudal-fin markings. Generally considered reef-safe, it is best kept as the only tang in a large, stable system and tends to be territorial toward similarly shaped tankmates.
careNotes:
The Whitecheek Tang (Acanthurus nigricans) is sometimes also sold as the Goldrim or Gold-Rimmed Surgeonfish. A large, established reef or FOWLR system of around 500 L (130 gal) or more is generally advised to give this active swimmer adequate territory and stable water.

This species is commonly considered moderately difficult to keep. It tends to be sensitive during acclimation and is reported to be relatively susceptible to ich and Head and Lateral Line Erosion (HLLE) — clean, highly oxygenated water and a vitamin-rich, algae-heavy diet are generally advised to mitigate both. Adding only to mature systems (around a year or older) tends to improve long-term success.

Temperature is generally advised in the range of 23–27°C, with standard reef parameters applying throughout (SG 1.020–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12).

Dietary emphasis should be on marine algae — dried nori, spirulina-based foods, and grazing on live rock — supplemented sparingly with meaty foods. Adequate algal intake has been reported to strengthen the immune system and reduce conspecific aggression.

Temperament is generally peaceful toward unrelated reef fish, but the species tends to be territorial toward other Acanthurus tangs and similarly shaped surgeonfishes. It is best kept singly, or with four or more tangs simultaneously in a very large aquarium to diffuse aggression. The species is particularly known to fight with the closely related Powder Brown Tang (A. japonicus), with which it hybridizes in the wild.

Like all acanthurids, this species possesses a sharp caudal-peduncle scalpel used for defense; it is not venomous but can inflict lacerations and should be handled with care during transfers.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding of Acanthurus nigricans has not been documented in available sources. In the wild it is a broadcast spawner and forms a species complex with three other Acanthurus species (notably A. japonicus, A. achilles, and A. leucosternon) with which it has been reported to hybridize. Spawning in home aquaria is considered highly unlikely given the tank volumes required and the absence of reported successes in the hobby literature.

The original collection root URL provided by the controller did not surface a direct species listing; the product page was located via web search.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Acanthuridae / Acanthuriformes), adult size (36 cm fork length, wild), native range (Indo-Pacific and eastern Pacific), preferred temperature (24.5–28.8°C), and IUCN status (Least Concern). Wild-data max size higher than captive hobby sources; both incorporated into adult-size range.

additional.notes: Used for taxonomic confirmation, common-name synonyms (Goldrim, Velvet Surgeon, Yellowrimmed), and species-complex hybridization notes with A. japonicus, A. achilles, and A. leucosternon. Husbandry data not documented.

additional.name: AquariumDomain
additional.notes: Confirmed temperature (72–79°F), pH (8.1–8.4), SG (1.020–1.025), dKH (8–12), care level (moderate), reef-compatibility, and territoriality toward other tangs. Minimum tank listed as 70 gal — lower than other sources; included in spread.

additional.name: FreshMarine
additional.notes: Confirmed adult size (up to 10 in / 25 cm), water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025), herbivorous diet with nori emphasis, and disease susceptibility during acclimation. Tank size listed as 75–100 gal.

additional.notes: Designated secondary returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch; substituted FishBase, Wikipedia, AquariumDomain, and FreshMarine as alternative cross-verification sources per playbook fallback rule.

fish.breedingNotes: No captive breeding records found. Broadcast spawner in the wild; hybridizes with closely related Acanthurus species.
